119914.explanation: In important decisions, residents’ voices must be heard directly. At Samen Beter, we believe that residents are the true stakeholders of the municipality. They live, work, and thrive here and feel the effects of municipal decisions every day.

119918.explanation: Residents who wish to generate sustainable energy collectively can already use various national and provincial subsidies. Additional municipal financial support is therefore not always necessary.

119919.explanation: Distribution centers provide employment and offer residents opportunities to work close to home, which benefits the local economy. Of course, not every location is suitable for this. Samen Beter believes distribution centers only belong on larger industrial sites.

119920.explanation: An asylum seeker center causes a sudden and strong growth of one core, putting pressure on livability, available education and health services, the sense of security, and can lead to considerable unrest among residents. Samen Beter believes national politics must take responsibility!

119921.explanation: Employers benefit from the efforts of seasonal workers and must therefore also take responsibility for their housing on company premises. This prevents housing in villages and cores from being occupied, while our own residents already struggle to find accommodation.

119922.explanation: A green environment is important and contributes to a pleasant and healthy living environment. However, that does not mean that every tree cut down must automatically be replaced by a new one. Such a rigid rule can be limiting in complex or exceptional situations.

119929.explanation: First, municipal lands must be utilized. Kangaroo and informal care homes should be possible on farmsteads.

119930.explanation: There are sufficient existing schemes, such as the Sports Fund in which the municipality invests. This enables everyone, regardless of income, to participate in sports and exercise without the municipality incurring extra costs. Samen Beter believes that sports should be accessible to everyone.

119933.explanation: Music education is important for the cultural and personal development of every child. Not all parents have the financial means to send their child to music lessons. Therefore, it is good that the municipality supports primary schools in organizing music lessons.

119934.explanation: All associations can apply for subsidies, and we want to maintain this. Associations are the heart of society. Every association, whether sporting or not, deserves support from the municipality!

119936.explanation: The key is that current enforcement officers are deployed efficiently and purposefully. BOAs should focus on what truly increases safety, such as preventing nuisance and proactively engaging with loitering youths, instead of enforcing minor and insignificant matters.

119937.explanation: We do not want a society where residents constantly feel watched. Privacy is a great value and must be protected. Exceptions could be made if safety in, for example, a shopping area demands it.

119942.explanation: There is a significant housing shortage in Nieuwkoop. By allowing holiday homes to be used as permanent residences, we can create extra living space without new building locations. This way, we make smart use of existing buildings and offer quicker solutions for those seeking housing.

119944.explanation: Those receiving social assistance benefits can provide a return service in the form of socially meaningful work. It enhances self-esteem, keeps people active, and prevents social isolation. There are always exceptions due to, for example, illness.

119945.explanation: The municipality should not automatically waive municipal taxes for residents with low incomes. There are sufficient other aids residents can rely on. Only when this is insufficient can the municipality, together with the resident, consider financial help and solutions.

119957.explanation: We believe social rental homes should primarily be available to our own residents. They too have a right to housing in the municipality where they live. When allocating housing, we give priority to people who already live here and are building their lives and futures in Nieuwkoop.

119959.explanation: By relocating the school to the sports fields, more multifunctional space is created that can be used flexibly for education and sports. The current school location can then be redeveloped into apartments. This way, we combine quality education with additional housing development in the Noorden core.

119962.explanation: Those who want to use a coffeeshop can do so in neighboring municipalities as well. A coffeeshop can also lead to nuisance and often lacks support from local residents. Safety, livability, and peace in the neighborhoods take priority.

119963.explanation: It is important in Nieuwkoop to have a good mix of homes, including social owner-occupied homes. The current housing distribution is sufficient and does not need to be increased to 35 percent. This way, we maintain a balanced housing market suitable for various target groups.

119965.explanation: Allowing the rental of individual rooms can help reduce the housing shortage. However, we must set the right conditions to prevent issues such as noise and parking nuisance.

119966.explanation: For Samen Beter, it does not matter whether a village is large or small; livability is important in all communities. By paying attention to facilities in all villages, we ensure balanced development and strong social cohesion throughout the municipality.

119967.explanation: Residents must be able to easily park near their homes and local facilities. By creating sufficient parking spaces in the villages, we increase accessibility, comfort, and livability for everyone in the municipality. Additional parking should only be provided where necessary!

119968.explanation: The crossing under the N231 at the roundabout by the gas station in Nieuwkoop is dangerous. With the arrival of Buytewech Noord, the new pool to be built, and the existing sports facilities, the traffic will only increase. It is therefore important that this traffic-unsafe spot is addressed!

119970.explanation: Vacant churches must receive a new purpose for social objectives or housing. It is important that village views are preserved: the inside of the church can be modified, but the outside and its characteristic appearance must remain intact.

119971.explanation: Allocating extra funds to ensure all bus lines run in the evenings and weekends is not the municipality’s task. However, we do find it important to invest in public transport so that our villages remain accessible to young and old who do not have a driver’s license.

119914.explanation: The VVD believes in representative democracy and is therefore not in favor of referenda. Council members are elected by residents to make important decisions, to thoroughly study all arguments and interests, and to carefully weigh everything.

119918.explanation: Involvement of residents in energy generation is important and necessary. The energy transition only succeeds with support. We therefore encourage local initiatives from residents, such as the area cooperative. The municipality supports in various ways, including with subsidies.

119919.explanation: We are against the establishment of large-scale distribution centers in our municipality. Such companies do not fit the open and green character of Nieuwkoop.

119920.explanation: We are currently searching with Kaag & Braassem for a location in the border area. This will probably be on Kaag & Braassem’s territory. Nieuwkoop complies with the law. This is not a choice, because otherwise the province chooses a location, and that will be a location intended for housing development.

119921.explanation: It is not realistic to house labor migrants everywhere on private property. Where possible, we encourage this. Making it a condition could cause problems for Nieuwkoop entrepreneurs who depend on labor migrants.

119922.explanation: We want to maintain the current number of trees at least at the current level, and preferably plant more. A healthy living environment requires the preservation of greenery.

119929.explanation: Allowing housing construction on all agricultural land is unrealistic, and the Province does not cooperate with this either. Where possible, we provide space for small-scale initiatives from individuals and, as a municipality, look at locations adjacent to village boundaries for new housing development.

119930.explanation: Income policy is not the municipality’s concern; that is the responsibility of the national government. However, we do have the Youth and Adult Sports & Culture Funds, which aim to support people with low incomes so they can participate in society.

119933.explanation: Music education greatly contributes to children's development. We want to continue the pilot for music education in primary schools and look at long-term co-financing options. Financing is not the municipality's responsibility alone.

119934.explanation: Associations must also keep their finances in order. The municipality often already contributes in the form of subsidies. (Too) easily providing financial support sets a precedent and may cost the municipality a lot of money. After all, the municipality is not a safety net for structural deficits.

119936.explanation: To increase safety and enforce against illegal situations, a strong number of BOAs is necessary, especially since more tasks may be assigned to BOAs in the future. Safety is a core task of the government.

119937.explanation: If camera surveillance can be proven to contribute to increased safety, this is a good and justified measure.

119942.explanation: VVD Nieuwkoop has been in favor of legalizing permanent residence for years. We believe balancing payments should be simple and modest. The contribution must be realistic because residents on the parks already face many costs, such as road maintenance.

119944.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits can certainly be expected to contribute to society, for example through volunteer work. Participating in society is not an optional choice.

119945.explanation: We find it indefensible to automatically waive taxes. This can only happen in seriously problematic situations, for example when problems would otherwise worsen. We use flexible payment arrangements and provide support, for example through Schuldhulpmaatje (debt assistance buddy).

119957.explanation: We support seeking alternative forms of housing for status holders, such as the use of flex-units. Giving priority to status holders while local residents wait years on the housing list is difficult to justify.

119959.explanation: In the long term, we want to realize a new IKC, probably near the sports fields. In the short term, we will start renewing the gym hall. By relocating the gym hall now, the beginning of a multifunctional sports and education facility emerges for the future.

119962.explanation: According to the VVD, coffeeshops do not belong in our villages but rather in urban environments.

119963.explanation: We find 30 percent social rental in new construction (a provincial obligation) more than sufficient. We want to focus primarily on affordable owner-occupied and (mid-range) rental homes, for all ages. More social rental is neither necessary nor desirable.

119965.explanation: We want to utilize all solutions that can help combat the housing shortage. Moreover, there are more and more one-person households and many singles living in large single-family homes, which could possibly be a solution.

119966.explanation: We support encouraging meetings and connections between residents. Every village should have a place where this is possible, such as a multifunctional community building.

119967.explanation: Not being able to park in your neighborhood or district is a major frustration. Where parking pressure is too high, we will therefore add extra parking spaces where possible.

119968.explanation: A third bike tunnel is needed to improve the safety of cyclists and pedestrians, for example when they are heading to primary schools or to clubs. With the arrival of the new swimming pool and the construction of Buytewech-North, the urgency only increases.

119970.explanation: Church buildings often define the look of our villages. We want to cherish this. At the same time, transformation offers opportunities, for example for housing, hospitality, or other recreational functions. To make this happen, the municipality must be much more proactive in consulting with churches.

119971.explanation: The Province, not the municipality, subsidizes public transport. We advocate for better public transport through lobbying in the region and at the provincial level, and by engaging with Qbuzz, for example to increase the frequency of bus lines, so that buses run more often.

119914.explanation: CDA Nieuwkoop believes that complex decisions do not belong in a yes/no referendum. The municipal council is elected to carefully weigh interests, with opportunities for input through participation.

119918.explanation: Local energy initiatives accelerate the energy transition, increase support, and keep proceeds within the community. Municipal support makes this feasible.

119919.explanation: Economic activity is important but must fit the municipality. A large distribution center does not suit the livability of our villages and causes problems with traffic safety and pressure on access roads.

119920.explanation: CDA finds it important that we all contribute our part. This can be done in many ways by smart collaboration with other municipalities for reception locations and making small-scale contributions.

119921.explanation: Housing on the employer’s own premises prevents nuisance, increases employer responsibility, and ensures better living conditions for seasonal workers.

119922.explanation: Replanting felled trees is important for a municipality in the Green Heart. It does not have to be in the same place, as long as the total greenery remains and is strengthened where possible.

119929.explanation: There is a shortage of homes and building sites. This offers a future perspective for house hunters and farmers. It helps strengthen the livability of the countryside, provided it is carefully integrated into the landscape and fits within clear agreements.

119930.explanation: Sport promotes health, social interaction, and participation in society. Financial support prevents exclusion and helps people on social assistance remain active and involved.

119933.explanation: Music education is valuable, but choice and a broad offering matter. Municipal support should not go solely to music and especially be intended for children who otherwise cannot afford it.

119934.explanation: The municipality can intervene if a sports club is future-proof, has enough volunteers, a realistic plan, and a healthy budget. Support should not become a bottomless pit without perspective.

119936.explanation: More enforcement officers (boa’s) ensure visibility, approachability, and faster action against nuisance. This increases the feeling of safety in villages and neighborhoods.

119937.explanation: Camera surveillance can help prevent and solve incidents. This must be done in a targeted manner, with clear rules and respect for privacy.

119942.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation. Permanent residence puts pressure on facilities and makes enforcement difficult.

119944.explanation: Work to capacity helps people maintain structure, self-esteem, and social contacts. This way, everyone contributes their part.

119945.explanation: Debt relief prevents people with low incomes from falling deeper into financial trouble.

119957.explanation: The pressure on the housing market is already high. Priority increases tension and feelings of inequality. Housing must be fairly distributed based on urgency, waiting time, and ties to the municipality. The municipality must continue to fulfill its legal duties.

119959.explanation: Investing in school buildings is investing in the future. Good schools make villages attractive to families and strengthen livability and the local economy.

119962.explanation: A coffeeshop offers limited economic benefits and can lead to nuisance and extra enforcement costs. It does not fit the character of the municipality.

119963.explanation: This is needed to keep housing affordable for starters, families, and people with lower incomes. A healthy housing mix ensures social and economic balance.

119965.explanation: Room rental can help with the housing shortage for starters and young people. Clear rules are needed to prevent nuisance and exploitation.

119966.explanation: Not every village has the same needs or scale. Investment must fit support, use, and financial feasibility.

119967.explanation: Good accessibility is important for shops, healthcare, and clubs. Too few parking spaces harms the local economy.

119968.explanation: A third bike tunnel contributes to traffic safety but also costs a lot to construct. The costs are so high that the CDA chooses to first address other traffic bottlenecks.

119970.explanation: Repurposing prevents dilapidation and preserves valuable buildings. It can also offer new economic and social opportunities.

119971.explanation: Good public transport increases accessibility for work, care, and social life. That strengthens participation and the local economy.

119914.explanation: Residents must be actively involved in developing plans for their environment and policies so that their input can be considered in decision-making. The risk of a referendum is raising false expectations.

119918.explanation: These kinds of initiatives should be supported as well as possible, also to obtain subsidies from the national government. Local ownership of larger generation is essential. Those who cannot afford to invest certainly also need help.

119919.explanation: Nieuwkoop is not suitable as a location for distribution centers and this is also undesirable because of the impact on the landscape and pressure on the roads.

119920.explanation: We must follow the current dispersal law. From a humanitarian perspective, we prefer small-scale reception spread across the centers. Residents must be closely involved in decision-making about this. Cooperation with, for example, Kaag and Braassem is important.

119921.explanation: Housing labor migrants at employers is an option provided the rights and position of workers are well protected, for example in cases of dismissal. This is important to prevent abuses.

119922.explanation: Every opportunity to plant new trees must be seized, especially when trees are removed.

119929.explanation: Transforming unused buildings also for housing must be further developed. Building homes is already possible as long as it fits within spatial possibilities (Space for Space) and the rules of the Environmental Plans.

119930.explanation: We support families with a minimum income with the Sports Pass. This is already policy, but we believe it can be expanded to a 130 percent income limit. A personal contribution of, for example, 30 percent is desirable.

119933.explanation: Music broadens culture but is inaccessible to many children. By organizing this, it becomes accessible to everyone.

119934.explanation: Sports clubs receive very good support. Where there are bottlenecks, there are tools to assist them. Facilities are very important, and we as a municipality provide much support here, and that must continue.

119936.explanation: For residents, it is important that enforcement officers (boa’s) are also visible, as this increases the feeling of safety.

119937.explanation: Using camera surveillance at specific locations in the municipality of Nieuwkoop can be an effective tool as an emergency measure against severe nuisance and should primarily be temporary.

119942.explanation: A strategy to enable permanent residence is only allowed for the seven parks in Zevenhoven. It is not permitted elsewhere.

119944.explanation: The municipality of Nieuwkoop has a limited number of social assistance recipients (about 300 people in Nieuwkoop). Natuurlijk Nieuwkoop believes encouraging participation and offering opportunities is far more effective than imposing an obligation.

119945.explanation: We will continue to support residents who are struggling. Forgiving debts in acute poverty situations is an act of decency and compassion.

119957.explanation: This concerns status holders, people with a residence permit. They must be able to transition from asylum seeker centers (AZCs), and all municipalities must contribute their share. Currently, less than 10 percent of all newly available social rental homes are allocated to status holders.

119959.explanation: In new developments, relocating the school can be part of a long-term plan. However, financial interests must also be carefully weighed.

119962.explanation: A cannabis shop also causes some nuisance and lowers the threshold for use. It is difficult because if you don’t allow it, you effectively shift the issue to neighboring municipalities.

119963.explanation: More social rental homes must be built in the coming years to accommodate people seeking housing. The exact percentage is not that important. People must be able to find a suitable and affordable home.

119965.explanation: Under the umbrella of housing corporations, this can contribute to housing residents. In private rentals, it can lead to undesirable situations.

119966.explanation: All large villages must have a viable village center. The presence of a supermarket is essential for this. Natuurlijk Nieuwkoop will actively work towards this.

119967.explanation: When (re)designing neighborhoods in our cores, opportunities and necessity must be carefully considered. Not all neighborhoods are suitable. In new neighborhood developments, it must be ensured that increased parking pressure is feasible.

119968.explanation: This is an increasingly important desire given developments on the Buytewech side of the core. The swimming pool, housing construction, and a possible relocation of sports hall De Steupel make this necessary.

119970.explanation: Those churches must receive a new purpose. Stakeholders and developers must develop new plans. The municipality cooperates within certain limits. Preserving the iconic buildings in our cores is desirable.

119971.explanation: Especially the line to Woerden and lines within the municipality need improved service. It must be considered whether Nieuwkoop can allocate sufficient funds for this.

119914.explanation: D66 wants to maintain ongoing dialogue with residents, not just occasional contact. Therefore, we continuously organize evenings in the villages. A referendum is an expensive method and only involves residents briefly.

119918.explanation: It's good that residents want to jointly pursue sustainability. D66 Nieuwkoop supports energy cooperatives with financial assistance so that people without a large budget can participate. This accelerates the energy transition, keeps proceeds local, and together we build a cleaner Nieuwkoop.

119919.explanation: Jobs are welcome, but mega warehouses along our roads consume space, landscape, and cause extra truck traffic. D66 Nieuwkoop wants business activity that fits: local, sustainable, and on existing sites.

119920.explanation: Due to the Dispersion Act, Nieuwkoop is obliged to arrange shelter; if we don’t, the province takes over control. D66 therefore chooses an asylum seeker center with clear agreements about safety, guidance, and community support.

119921.explanation: Seasonal workers are needed, but their housing must be properly arranged. D66 believes employers should take responsibility and arrange decent, safe housing on their own premises, with clear rules. This prevents nuisance and protects workers.

119922.explanation: Trees make Nieuwkoop greener, cooler, and healthier. Felling must mean replanting to avoid losing green space bit by bit. Where necessary, this can be with multiple young trees or better locations so the greenery truly returns and becomes stronger.

119929.explanation: Housing is urgent, also for (farming) families. But new houses in the countryside mean sealing land and pressure on the landscape and nature. D66 does choose for residential use on farms, splitting and shared housing on existing farms, and primarily building in the village centers.

119930.explanation: Participation should not be a luxury. D66 Nieuwkoop wants people on social assistance to be able to engage in sports, as it improves health, networks, and job opportunities. Through a simple scheme (like the Youth Fund/adult fund), we help targeted groups so money is not a barrier.

119933.explanation: Music lessons are more than a hobby: they provide confidence and opportunities. D66 Nieuwkoop wants every primary school child to participate in music lessons at schools, together with local associations. This keeps it affordable and accessible.

119934.explanation: We want strong sports clubs, but continuous support with municipal funds is not always fair or sustainable. D66 Nieuwkoop chooses help that works: advice, sustainability improvements, use of facilities, and support in temporary emergencies. This keeps clubs healthy and fees fair.

119936.explanation: Safety begins with visibility on the streets. D66 Nieuwkoop wants more enforcement officers so that nuisance and unsafe traffic are addressed more quickly and there is more preventive contact with youth. This relieves the police and keeps living pleasant.

119937.explanation: Safety is important, but cameras are a heavy measure and affect privacy. D66 Nieuwkoop first chooses what really works: good lighting, neighborhood officers, and addressing hotspots. Camera surveillance only temporarily and targeted if other measures fall short.

119942.explanation: Housing shortages are severe; sometimes a vacation home is someone's only residence. D66 Nieuwkoop wants to allow registration where possible: safely, fitting the zoning plan, and with agreements with the park and neighbors. This provides certainty without losing recreation.

119944.explanation: Many people want to participate but sometimes need a push or rhythm. D66 Nieuwkoop supports socially meaningful work as a reciprocal obligation, provided it fits someone's situation, does not displace others, and includes good guidance towards paid work or education.

119945.explanation: Getting by is already difficult with a low income. D66 Nieuwkoop wants to maintain debt forgiveness: this prevents debts and stress and keeps help available for those who really need it. Fair and humane policy.

119957.explanation: Waiting for a social rental home is frustrating; priority feels unfair. However, Nieuwkoop has the legal task to house status holders so people can move out of shelters. D66 Nieuwkoop therefore supports targeted priority and extra construction.

119959.explanation: Every parent wants a safe, healthy school. Renewing Antonius in Noorden costs money but is necessary in the long term. D66 Nieuwkoop opts for a future-proof building: good indoor climate, energy-efficient, and room for the best education.

119962.explanation: Concerned about nuisance? D66 Nieuwkoop chooses a regulated coffee shop: clear rules, a location at a distance from schools, supervision, and enforcement. This takes trade off the streets, makes use better manageable, and increases safety.

119963.explanation: Housing shortage affects everyone; 35 percent social housing sounds high. But Nieuwkoop currently has about 21 percent social rental and needs to reach 30. This requires catching up in new construction. D66 chooses mixed neighborhoods so starters and lower-income people can live here.

119965.explanation: House sharing can relieve the tight market; at the same time, there are concerns about nuisance. D66 Nieuwkoop supports room rental with clear rules: quality requirements, a maximum per home, good registration, and enforcement. This helps starters and students without burdening neighborhoods.

119966.explanation: Meeting in one’s own village keeps associations and communities strong. D66 Nieuwkoop wants an accessible, multifunctional center per large village—preferably through smart use/expansion of existing buildings.

119967.explanation: Not necessarily extra parking everywhere, but where the shortage is structural. In villages with chronic parking pressure, we add spaces, preferably with green, permeable paving. For accessibility and greenery.

119968.explanation: Crossing the N231 currently feels unsafe for many people. A third bicycle tunnel at the roundabout (gas station) makes routes more direct and safer, especially for students. D66 Nieuwkoop supports this and wants to work with the province on realization.

119970.explanation: Vacant churches often evoke mixed feelings: heritage and vacancy. D66 Nieuwkoop wants active repurposing, together with owners and residents. This preserves buildings and gives them a function that strengthens the village, such as culture, housing, or meeting places.

119971.explanation: Public transport in the evening and weekend seems expensive with low occupancy. Yet it keeps villages accessible for youth, elderly, and employees without cars. D66 Nieuwkoop wants extra funds to guarantee a basic service, with smart demand-driven transport where needed.

119914.explanation: The municipal council is already a representation, and in addition, there are opportunities for residents to express themselves about certain policies. There are also village councils.

119918.explanation: This also helps the municipality to comply with the set requirements.

119919.explanation: Apart from the fact that there is hardly any space for this in Nieuwkoop, you should not want this either.

119920.explanation: Nothing must be done here, but we must follow what the government imposes on us.

119921.explanation: Together as a sector, we must put our heads together to get this facility up and running and maintain it.

119922.explanation: Nothing must be done here, but where possible the opportunity must be utilized.

119929.explanation: Where this is possible from a planning perspective, that is fine.

119930.explanation: If there is a need for it, but with control to ensure it is actually used.

119933.explanation: Music lessons are fine, but not necessarily financed by the municipality.

119934.explanation: There is good policy on this, and self-reliance is very important to keep the association afloat.

119936.explanation: There is no reason for concern and there are few unsafe situations.

119937.explanation: We do not want interference. There are also no fixed locations where cameras could be useful.

119942.explanation: The municipality is already fully engaged with that.

119944.explanation: It is logical that there must be a reciprocal obligation. It must somewhat fit the individuals receiving it.

119945.explanation: The municipality already has good policy for that.

119959.explanation: Only if there is also a new plan for the sports hall.

119962.explanation: Why would we want to bring that within the municipality?

119963.explanation: With our current goal of 30 percent, we are already leading the way.

119965.explanation: The entire environment is not designed for that.

119966.explanation: That is a challenge, and who determines what constitutes a 'larger' core?

119970.explanation: The municipality must think along about a suitable solution that does justice to the building.

119971.explanation: Only if there is a need.

statements.title: A third bicycle tunnel should be constructed under the N231 at the roundabout by the gas station in Nieuwkoop.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Nieuwkoop should employ more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Nieuwkoop should allocate funds so that all bus lines also run in the evenings and on weekends.
The residents of Nieuwkoop should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Nieuwkoop moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Nieuwkoop must renovate Antonius Primary School in the Noorden village center.

titleParts.text: Nieuwkoop moet Basisschool Antonius in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Nieuwkoop.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: All larger villages in the municipality should have their own village centre.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: At least 35% of newly built homes in Nieuwkoop should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Nieuwkoop moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality must ensure that vacant churches are given a new use.
